events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes "mixed old/new belts on multi-groove" on a production floor, in-service. This is a known defect: mixing old and new belts on a multi-groove sheave causes uneven tension, rapid wear, and premature failure. This is non-compliant with manufacturer guides which typically say replace all belts as a matched set.

Decision: fail (non-compliant/dangerous condition).
Risk: medium or high - could cause belt failure, equipment downtime.
Component conditions: degraded (old belts degraded), non-compliant (mixed set).
Percent complete: in-service, so 100.
Confidence high.

  "rationale": "On multi-groove drives, belts must be replaced as a matched set. Mixing old and new belts results in uneven length and tension, causing the new belts to be overloaded while the old belts slip, leading to rapid wear, heat generation, vibration, and premature failure of the entire set.",
